entities.name: COVID-19 PANDEMIC
entities.description: Global health crisis that impacted household food security and income

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient, affordable, and nutritious food

entities.name: COPING STRATEGIES
entities.description: Methods adopted by households to mitigate the impact of food insecurity

entities.name: HYDERABAD, INDIA
entities.description: The geographic location where the study on food security was conducted

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD SURVEY
entities.description: Data collection method used to gather information before and during the pandemic

entities.name: LOGISTIC REGRESSION
entities.description: Statistical method used to analyze the factors affecting food security status

entities.name: FOOD INSECURITY EXPERIENCE SCALE (FIES)
entities.description: A standardized scale used as a dependent variable to measure food insecurity

entities.name: SECTOR OF EMPLOYMENT
entities.description: The professional field of the primary income earner, influencing food security

entities.name: CONSUMPTION-SMOOTHING STRATEGIES
entities.description: Specific coping mechanisms used to maintain consumption during income shocks

entities.name: CREDIT
entities.description: Financial assistance from formal and informal sources used for consumption smoothing

entities.name: SAVINGS
entities.description: Liquidated financial assets used to maintain food security

entities.name: STORED FOOD
entities.description: Food reserves used by households with mild food insecurity to smooth consumption

entities.name: INCOME SHOCK
entities.description: A sudden loss or reduction of income caused by the pandemic

entities.name: UNEMPLOYMENT
entities.description: The state of lacking a job, which significantly influenced food security status

entities.name: SOCIAL PROTECTION POLICIES
entities.description: Policy responses designed to mitigate pandemic impacts and improve food security governance

entities.name: URBAN AND PERI-URBAN AREAS
entities.description: The specific residential settings in Hyderabad analyzed in the study
